resources.description: Search for or read a FHIR Patient resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, you MUST supply one of these minimum data sets — name alone is never sufficient: (1) identifier (MRN, FHIR ID, or other system|value identifier), (2) given + family + birthdate, or (3) given + family + legal-sex (or gender) + telecom. Always ask the user for enough information to satisfy one of these sets before searching.

searchParams._id: Patient resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.name: Any part of the patient's name — ignored when family or given is also provided
searchParams.birthdate: Date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format
searchParams.gender: Administrative gender: female, male, other, unknown
searchParams.legal-sex: Legal sex — takes precedence over gender when both are provided: female, male, nonbinary, x, unknown
searchParams.telecom: Phone number or email address (prefix # to skip phone validation, + for international)
searchParams.identifier: Business identifier in system|value format (e.g. MRN, SSN, FHIR ID)

resources.description: Search for or read a FHIR Observation resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, patient is required. Optionally filter by category (e.g. laboratory, vital-signs, core-characteristics) and/or code (LOINC). Use category=core-characteristics with code 76516-4 (gestational age) or 8339-4 (birth weight) for core characteristic lookups.

searchParams._id: Observation resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.patient: Patient resource ID or reference — required for search

resources.description: Search for or read a FHIR Condition resource covering both problem list items and encounter diagnoses. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient or encounter (at least one required). Use category=problem-list-item for the patient's problem list (clinical-status: active, resolved, inactive) or category=encounter-diagnosis for diagnoses documented during encounters. Omitting category returns both types.

searchParams._id: Condition resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.patient: Patient resource ID or reference — required unless encounter is provided
searchParams.encounter: Encounter resource ID — use instead of patient to get diagnoses for a specific encounter
searchParams.category: problem-list-item for problem list entries, encounter-diagnosis for encounter diagnoses; omit for both
searchParams.clinical-status: Clinical status: active, resolved, or inactive

resources.description: Search for or read a FHIR Encounter resource. Provide an _id for direct read, or search by patient, status, class, or date.

searchParams._id: Encounter resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.date: Encounter date or date range (e.g. ge2024-01-01)

searchControls._count: Maximum number of results per page — controls response size and token usage
searchControls._sort: Sort order for results (e.g. -date for newest first, birthdate for oldest first)
searchControls._summary: Return summarized resources: true (metadata only), text (text + id + meta), count (count only), data (remove text)
searchControls._elements: Comma-separated list of element names to include in each resource (e.g. name,birthDate)
searchControls._include: Include referenced resources in the response Bundle (e.g. Observation:patient)
searchControls._revinclude: Include resources that reference the matched resources (e.g. Provenance:target)
